summary: Compared the current homepage against the upstream conference template and found that the largest alignment gaps are the hero/banner treatment, the navigation rhythm, and several homepage-only CSS customizations that replace the template’s plain content flow with a more custom landing-page look. The minimum Builder change set is to restore the template’s image-backed banner and navigation proportions, then keep the ATLAS copy while removing the custom callout styling rather than rewriting content.
work_completed: Read the result packet schema and the referenced product/design docs for alignment constraints.
Produced a prioritized gap analysis and a minimum follow-up plan for Builder.

findings.message: Hero/banner structure is the biggest visual divergence. Upstream `index.html:17-25` uses a photographic banner image plus overlaid `.top-left` and `.bottom-right` text, while current `index.html:20-31` removes the `` entirely and introduces a custom `.banner-panel` gradient block. The CSS shift is equally large: upstream `assets/main.css:79-87` assumes an image-backed banner with a white title strip, but current `assets/main.css:95-139` replaces that with a full-bleed gradient panel, white title text, and a restyled subtitle/year treatment. This materially changes the template aesthetic.

Navigation no longer follows the template’s information architecture or spacing pattern. Upstream `index.html:27-45` presents a five-item site nav (`Home`, `Registration`, `Program`, `Directions`, `Flyer`) and upstream `assets/main.css:89-91` sizes it as an `800px` table with `20%` cells at `1.67em`. Current `index.html:33-48` uses four in-page anchor links only, and current `assets/main.css:148-158` widens the table to `900px`, drops cells to `25%`, and reduces type to `1.35em`. Even if anchor navigation stays, the current nav reads as a custom section jump bar rather than the recognizable template nav.

Homepage content flow has been converted from the template’s simple `h2` + paragraph stream into a more bespoke landing-page composition. Current `index.html:50-127` adds `<main>`, a standalone `.subtitle-block`, a scoped bullet list, and emphasized `.quote` / `.question` callouts. Those are supported by new CSS in `assets/main.css:141-168`. Upstream homepage content at `index.html:47-92` is plain heading/paragraph/table content with no special callout components. This is not a correctness issue, but it is the main structural reason the page no longer feels template-derived.

Several upstream baseline CSS behaviors were removed or altered, which weakens template fidelity beyond the homepage copy swap. Current stylesheet drops upstream link underline behavior inside body copy (`assets/main.css` upstream `51`, missing in current), removes `.title2` styling by eliminating that markup entirely (upstream `assets/main.css:84-87`, current HTML `22-25`), changes list treatment from plain text blocks to indented bullets (`current assets/main.css:70-78` vs upstream `52-53`), and shortens footer spacing (`current 170-174` vs upstream `118`). These are smaller than the hero/nav changes but collectively push the site away from the upstream visual rhythm.

Current page metadata is more complete than upstream. Current `index.html:4-17` adds charset, viewport, stronger description, and better keywords, while upstream `index.html:5-12` includes a `<base>` tag, favicon link, and simpler metadata. These metadata improvements do not need to be rolled back to restore template alignment.

The current single-page anchor model is consistent with the repo spec in `docs/product-specs/workshop-website.md`, which states that the first release is a static single-page site and that navigation is in-page anchor navigation. Builder should therefore restore template alignment primarily through banner/layout/styling changes, not by forcing a return to the upstream multi-page site structure.

follow_up_recommendations: Builder should restore the upstream banner pattern first: reintroduce the template-style image-backed hero markup from upstream `index.html:17-25`, keep the ATLAS title/date copy, and remove the custom `.banner-panel` treatment from current `assets/main.css:95-139`.

Builder should reset navigation to the upstream proportions from `assets/main.css:89-91` while preserving in-page anchors: use the template table width/type scale, and consider adding a fifth placeholder or future-facing nav item only if needed to recover the template’s visual rhythm without inventing new content.

Builder should keep all ATLAS section copy already written in current `index.html:55-126`, but simplify presentation by removing `.subtitle-block`, `.quote`, and `.question` styling and letting those sections render with mostly upstream paragraph/list rules. This is the minimum change that restores template feel without discarding content.
